evals.name: Pyramidal Horn with Waveguide Feed
evals.description: Tests extrusion workflow with taper for horn flare and feed placement
evals.prompt: Build a pyramidal horn antenna for 10 GHz using customAntenna. Start with a WR-90 rectangular waveguide section, extrude a flared horn from the open end using a tapered extrusion. Add a feed inside the waveguide. Show the geometry and mesh it. Display the complete MATLAB script in your response.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses extrudeLinear with taper for the horn flare
assertions.expected: The displayed MATLAB script contains extrudeLinear called with a Scale parameter to produce the flared horn shape. Must NOT use extrudeRotate (that's for bodies of revolution, not rectangular horns).

assertions.description: Adds a feed in the waveguide section
assertions.expected: The displayed MATLAB script contains createFeed to define a feed point inside or at the waveguide section. Must NOT omit the feed entirely — customAntenna requires a defined feed.

assertions.description: Meshes the antenna explicitly
assertions.expected: The displayed MATLAB script contains a call to mesh() on the custom antenna object. Must NOT skip meshing.

assertions.description: Does not use antenna.* namespace with customAntenna
assertions.expected: antenna.Rectangle|antenna.Circle|antenna.Polygon

evals.name: Rectangular Patch with Strip Feed and Ground Plane
evals.description: Tests patch antenna construction with strip feed and ground plane using customAntenna
evals.prompt: Design a rectangular patch antenna for 2.4 GHz with a strip feed and ground plane using customAntenna. The patch should be above the ground plane. Show the geometry and mesh it. Display the complete MATLAB script in your response.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Creates a strip feed connecting to the patch
assertions.expected: The displayed MATLAB script contains a strip feed — either a narrow rectangle shape on the surface approaching the patch edge, a vertical strip connecting ground to patch, or createFeed at a connection point. Must NOT omit the feed entirely — customAntenna requires a defined feed.

assertions.description: Defines a ground plane below the patch
assertions.expected: The displayed MATLAB script creates a ground plane (shape.Rectangle or shape.Polygon) positioned below the patch. Must NOT omit the ground plane — a patch antenna requires a ground reference.

assertions.description: Meshes the antenna explicitly
assertions.expected: The displayed MATLAB script contains a call to mesh() on the custom antenna object. Must NOT skip meshing.

assertions.description: Does not use antenna.* namespace with customAntenna
assertions.expected: antenna.Rectangle|antenna.Circle|antenna.Polygon

evals.name: Cross-Shaped Slot Antenna from mm Dimensions
evals.description: Tests createHole for 2D boolean operations, feed at geometric discontinuity, and unit conversion from mm to meters
evals.prompt: Create a slot antenna using customAntenna: a 120mm x 120mm ground plane with a cross-shaped slot (two perpendicular slots, each 50mm x 3mm) cut at the center. Add a feed at one slot edge. Mesh the antenna and compute impedance at 2.4 GHz.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses createHole or subtraction for cutting slots in 2D ground plane
assertions.expected: createHole called to cut slot shapes from the ground plane, OR boolean subtraction (-) is used, OR the slotted ground is defined directly as a shape.Polygon with the slot outline. The skill teaches that createHole is preferred for 2D shapes. All approaches are acceptable if code executes without error.

assertions.description: Cross shape formed by combining two perpendicular rectangles
assertions.expected: The cross-shaped slot is created from two perpendicular rectangles (one horizontal, one vertical, each 50mm x 3mm). These may be unioned before cutting (preferred for overlapping shapes) or cut separately if the geometry succeeds. Since code_executes passes, the geometry was handled correctly regardless of method.

assertions.description: Feed is placed at slot edge (geometric discontinuity)
assertions.expected: Feed location coordinates are at or near the edge of a slot (metal/air boundary). Evidence: output shows feed position at a slot endpoint or edge coordinate. Must NOT place feed in the middle of continuous metal far from any slot edge — delta-gap feeds require geometric discontinuity to produce realistic impedance.

assertions.description: Converts mm dimensions to meters for shape constructors
assertions.expected: All shape dimensions passed to shape.Rectangle, shape.Polygon, or other shape constructors are in meters (e.g., 0.12, 0.05, 0.003). Evidence: output shows coordinates/dimensions in meters, or feed position in meters. The prompt gives dimensions in mm but MATLAB shape properties require meters. Must NOT pass raw mm values (120, 50, 3) directly — that would create a 120-meter ground plane.

evals.name: Conical Horn via Body of Revolution
evals.description: Tests extrudeRotate for body of revolution, shape.Polygon profile definition, and circular feed creation
evals.prompt: Build a conical horn antenna for 5 GHz using customAntenna. You must use extrudeRotate to revolve a trapezoidal profile around the z-axis. The horn should transition from a 10mm radius waveguide opening to a 40mm radius aperture over 80mm length. Add a feed at the waveguide end. Show the geometry and mesh it.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses extrudeRotate with a 2D polygon profile to create the conical horn
assertions.expected: extrudeRotate is called to revolve a 2D trapezoidal profile around the z-axis to create the conical horn shape. Since code_executes passes and extrudeRotate produced valid geometry, the profile must be a valid shape.Polygon with vertices in the XY plane (z=0) — extrudeRotate errors otherwise. Evidence: output mentions extrudeRotate, revolution, revolving profile, trapezoidal profile, or body of revolution. Must NOT use extrudeLinear (that's for prismatic/rectangular shapes).

assertions.description: Adds a feed at the waveguide end of the horn
assertions.expected: A feed is created at or near the narrow waveguide end of the horn (at the 10mm radius end). Evidence: output mentions feed creation, createFeed, or feed location at waveguide section. Must NOT place the feed at the aperture (wide end) — feed excitation belongs at the waveguide input.

assertions.description: Does not use antenna.* namespace with customAntenna
assertions.expected: antenna.Rectangle|antenna.Circle|antenna.Polygon

evals.name: Dual-Feed Truncated-Corner Patch for Circular Polarization
evals.description: Tests shape.Polygon direct definition, multi-feed excitation with FeedVoltage and FeedPhase, and antenna.* namespace guardrail
evals.prompt: Design a truncated-corner square patch antenna for circular polarization at 3.5 GHz using customAntenna. The patch should be approximately 25mm x 25mm with two opposite corners truncated. Place two orthogonal feeds and excite them with 90-degree phase difference for CP. Show the geometry and mesh it.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Defines truncated patch as shape.Polygon directly or via successful boolean
assertions.expected: The truncated-corner patch is defined directly as a shape.Polygon with vertices describing the truncated outline, OR uses boolean subtraction that executes successfully. The skill teaches that direct Polygon definition is preferred when the final outline is simple to describe — fewer operations, no boolean failure modes. Both approaches are acceptable if code executes.

assertions.description: Configures 90-degree phase offset for circular polarization
assertions.expected: The two feeds are excited with 90-degree phase difference to generate circular polarization. Evidence includes ANY of: output mentions FeedPhase, 90° phase, phase offset, RHCP, LHCP, circular polarization excitation, or [0 90] phase values. Since code_executes passes and the agent describes CP excitation, the phase must be configured. Must NOT excite both feeds with the same phase — that produces linear polarization.

assertions.description: Sets FeedVoltage for dual-feed excitation
assertions.expected: FeedVoltage property is set with equal magnitude for both feeds (e.g., [1 1]) to ensure balanced excitation. Evidence: output mentions FeedVoltage or dual-feed voltage. Must NOT leave FeedVoltage as default [1] for a dual-feed antenna — that would only excite the first feed.

assertions.description: Creates two feed points for orthogonal excitation
assertions.expected: createFeed called with a 2-row location matrix [x1 y1 z1; x2 y2 z2] or called twice for two separate feed points. The two feeds must be at orthogonal positions on the patch (e.g., one offset along x-axis, other along y-axis) for CP generation. Evidence: output mentions two feeds, orthogonal positions, or dual-feed creation.

assertions.description: Does not use antenna.* namespace with customAntenna
assertions.expected: antenna.Rectangle|antenna.Circle|antenna.Polygon
